narration: Here's something wild — being poor actually costs you more money. Low-income Americans pay up to $1,300 extra a year for the same life. It's called the poverty premium.
visual: Two person silhouettes side by side: red LOW INCOME figure with '+$1,300' price tag bubble, green HIGH INCOME figure with '$0 EXTRA' tag. Counter animates from $0 to $1,300.

narration: Number one: food. Without cash for bulk packs, you pay 43% more per ounce at corner stores. That's $800 a year extra, just on groceries.
visual: Two shopping bags slide in from opposite sides. Small red bag: '$3.49/oz CORNER STORE'. Large green bag: '$0.99/oz BULK STORE'. '43% MORE EXPENSIVE' badge animates in.

narration: Number two: banking. No minimum balance? You pay $600 a year just to access your own money. Check-cashing fees, overdrafts — they charge you for being broke.
visual: Bank building SVG with dollar signs flying upward away from the building. Fee counter animates from $0 to $600/YR below the building.

narration: Number three: credit. Rich people borrow at 3% APR. Payday loans charge 400%. Same $500 loan — one costs twenty bucks, the other costs two thousand.
visual: Two storefronts side by side: blue BANK with '3% APR' badge, red PAYDAY LOANS storefront with APR counter animating from 0% to 400%. '133× MORE INTEREST' label appears.

narration: Number four: insurance. Just your zip code adds $500 a year to your car insurance. Same driver, same car — different address means a different financial fate.
visual: Two houses side by side with zip code labels: ZIP 60612 (red house, $2,100/yr) vs ZIP 90210 (blue house, $1,600/yr). '+$500/YR ZIP CODE PENALTY' counter animates.

narration: These four traps add up to $1,300 a year just for being poor. Knowing the system is step one to beating it. Follow for more money hacks.
visual: Animated bar chart with four rows — FOOD, BANKING, CREDIT, INSURANCE — growing left to right with dollar amounts. Total counter hits $1,300/YR. Red 'FOLLOW FOR MORE' CTA button.
